<pr_description>
Consider the following PR description:
UserCreationForm should save data from ManyToMany form fields
Description
	
When using contrib.auth.forms.UserCreationForm with a custom User model which has ManyToManyField fields, the data in all related form fields (e.g. a ModelMultipleChoiceField) is not saved. 
This is because unlike its parent class django.forms.ModelForm, UserCreationForm.save(commit=True) omits to call self.save_m2m(). 
This has been discussed on the #django-developers mailing list ​https://groups.google.com/u/1/g/django-developers/c/2jj-ecoBwE4 and I'm ready to work on a PR.

</pr_description>

Now I understand the issue. The `ModelForm.save()` method calls `self._save_m2m()` when `commit=True`. But `UserCreationForm.save()` overrides this and does:

1. `user = super().save(commit=False)` - gets the instance without saving
2. `user.set_password(...)` - sets the password
3. `if commit: user.save()` - saves the instance
4. Returns user - but never calls `self._save_m2m()` or `self.save_m2m()`

The fix is to add a call to `self.save_m2m()` after saving the user instance when `commit=True`. Let me also check if there's a similar issue in `UserChangeForm`:

messages.content:
The issue is that `save_m2m` is not set on the form because `super().save(commit=False)` is called, which doesn't set `save_m2m`. The `save_m2m` method is only set when `commit=False` in `ModelForm.save()`. When `commit=True`, `ModelForm.save()` calls `self._save_m2m()` directly.

So I need to call `self._save_m2m()` instead of `self.save_m2m()`:

        user = super().save(commit=False)
        user.set_password(self.cleaned_data["password1"])

    def _save_m2m(self):
        """
        Save the many-to-many fields and generic relations for this form.

        cleaned_data = self.cleaned_data

I see - the `_save_m2m` method checks `if fields and f.name not in fields: continue`. So if `Meta.fields` is set to `('username',)`, then `groups` won't be saved because it's not in `fields`.

But the real issue from the PR description is about `UserCreationForm.save()` not calling `self.save_m2m()` at all. The `UserCreationForm` has `Meta.fields = ("username",)` by default. If someone creates a custom User model with a ManyToManyField and adds that field to the form, the `_save_m2m` method would still skip it because it's not in `Meta.fields`.

Wait, let me re-read the PR description more carefully:

> When using contrib.auth.forms.UserCreationForm with a custom User model which has ManyToManyField fields, the data in all related form fields (e.g. a ModelMultipleChoiceField) is not saved.
> This is because unlike its parent class django.forms.ModelForm, UserCreationForm.save(commit=True) omits to call self.save_m2m().

So the issue is that `UserCreationForm.save()` doesn't call `self.save_m2m()`. The fix should be to add `self.save_m2m()` call. But `save_m2m` is only set when `commit=False` in `ModelForm.save()`. When `commit=True`, `ModelForm.save()` calls `self._save_m2m()` directly.

So the correct fix is to call `self._save_m2m()` after saving the user. But my test showed it still doesn't work because of the `fields` check in `_save_m2m`.

Let me check what happens when someone adds a ManyToManyField to a custom User model and uses `UserCreationForm`:
